---
title: "GenomicDistributions Tutorial — with RegionSet S4 class"
subtitle: >
  Following the [GenomicDistributions intro vignette](https://code.databio.org/GenomicDistributions/articles/intro.html),
  using the gtars RegionSet S4 class and its methods instead of drop-in wrappers.
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    code_folding: show
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
  fig.width = 10, fig.height = 5
)
```

# Getting started

GenomicDistributions provides a suite of functions for computing and
visualizing properties of genomic ranges. Every analysis follows the same
pattern: a **calc** function computes a summary, and a **plot** function
visualizes it.

**gtars** provides a `RegionSet` S4 class backed by Rust that exposes
these computations as S4 methods (`widths()`, `neighborDistances()`,
`nearestNeighbors()`, `distribution()`). The outputs are compatible with
GenomicDistributions' `plot*` functions.

```{r libraries}
library(GenomicDistributions)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(gtars)
```


## Load data

The tutorial uses the VISTA enhancers dataset bundled with
GenomicDistributions (1,339 human enhancer regions).

`RegionSet()` can load BED files directly — no GRanges intermediate needed:

```{r load-data}
queryFile <- system.file("extdata", "vistaEnhancers.bed.gz",
                         package = "GenomicDistributions")
query <- RegionSet(queryFile)
cat(length(query), "regions across",
    length(unique(as.data.frame(query)$chr)), "chromosomes\n")
```

We also need GRanges for GenomicDistributions comparisons and multi-query examples:

```{r load-granges}
query_gr <- rtracklayer::import(queryFile)
```

For multi-query examples, we create a shifted copy:

```{r query-list}
query2_gr <- GenomicRanges::shift(query_gr, 1e6)
queryList <- GRangesList(vistaEnhancers = query_gr, shifted = query2_gr)
query2 <- RegionSet(query2_gr)
```

We also load hg19 gene models (used in several sections below):

```{r gene-models}
geneModels <- GenomicDistributions::getGeneModels("hg19")
```


# Width distribution {.tabset}

`widths()` computes `end - start` for each region.

## GenomicDistributions

```{r gd-width}
gd_widths <- GenomicDistributions::calcWidth(query_gr)
plotQTHist(gd_widths)
```

## gtars RegionSet

```{r gt-width}
gt_widths <- widths(query)
plotQTHist(gt_widths)
```

## Comparison

```{r cmp-width}
cat("Exact match:", all(gd_widths == gt_widths), "\n")
plotQTHist(list("GenomicDistributions" = gd_widths, "gtars" = gt_widths))
```

##

Multi-query (GD only — gtars doesn't accept GRangesList directly):

```{r width-multi}
gd_widths2 <- GenomicDistributions::calcWidth(queryList)
plotQTHist(gd_widths2)
```

gtars multi-query (compute each RegionSet separately, combine into named list):

```{r width-multi-gtars}
plotQTHist(list(
  vistaEnhancers = widths(query),
  shifted = widths(query2)
))
```


# Chromosome distribution {.tabset}

`distribution()` bins the genome and counts region overlaps per bin.

## GenomicDistributions

```{r gd-chrom-bins}
gd_bins <- calcChromBinsRef(query_gr, "hg19")
plotChromBins(gd_bins)
```

## gtars RegionSet

`distribution()` returns a data.table with karyotype-sorted rows and
GD-compatible column names, so it can be passed directly to `plotChromBins`:

```{r gt-chrom-bins}
gt_bins <- distribution(query, 250L)
plotChromBins(gt_bins)
```

## Comparison

```{r cmp-chrom-bins}
cat("GD bins:", nrow(gd_bins), " gtars bins:", nrow(gt_bins), "\n")
```

##

Multi-query (GD only):

```{r chrom-bins-multi}
gd_bins2 <- calcChromBinsRef(queryList, "hg19")
plotChromBins(gd_bins2)
```


# Neighboring regions distance {.tabset}

`neighborDistances()` computes the gap between consecutive regions on each
chromosome.

## GenomicDistributions

```{r gd-neighbor}
gd_nd <- GenomicDistributions::calcNeighborDist(query_gr)
cat(length(gd_nd), "distances (negative/overlapping filtered out)\n")
plotNeighborDist(gd_nd)
```

## gtars RegionSet

```{r gt-neighbor}
gt_nd <- neighborDistances(query)
cat(length(gt_nd), "distances (signed, including", sum(gt_nd < 0), "overlaps)\n")

# Filter to non-negative for GD-compatible plotting
gt_nd_nonneg <- gt_nd[gt_nd >= 0]
plotNeighborDist(gt_nd_nonneg)
```

## Comparison

```{r cmp-neighbor}
cat("Non-negative values match:", all(gd_nd == gt_nd_nonneg), "\n")
plotNeighborDist(list("GenomicDistributions" = gd_nd, "gtars" = gt_nd_nonneg))
```

##

Multi-query:

```{r neighbor-multi}
setBFile <- system.file("extdata", "setB_100.bed.gz", package = "GenomicDistributions")
setB_gr <- rtracklayer::import(setBFile)

# GD handles GRangesList natively
queryList2 <- GRangesList(vistaEnhancers = query_gr, setB = setB_gr)
gd_nd2 <- GenomicDistributions::calcNeighborDist(queryList2)
plotNeighborDist(gd_nd2)
```

gtars multi-query (load BED directly, compute each RegionSet separately):

```{r neighbor-multi-gtars}
gt_nd_A <- neighborDistances(query)
gt_nd_B <- neighborDistances(RegionSet(setBFile))
plotNeighborDist(list(
  vistaEnhancers = gt_nd_A[gt_nd_A >= 0],
  setB = gt_nd_B[gt_nd_B >= 0]
))
```


# Nearest neighbor distance {.tabset}

`nearestNeighbors()` returns the minimum distance to the closest adjacent
neighbor for each region.

## GenomicDistributions

```{r gd-nn}
gd_nn <- GenomicDistributions::calcNearestNeighbors(query_gr)
cat(length(gd_nn), "values\n")
plotNeighborDist(as.integer(gd_nn), Nneighbors = TRUE)
```

## gtars RegionSet

```{r gt-nn}
gt_nn <- nearestNeighbors(query)
cat(length(gt_nn), "values (one per region)\n")
plotNeighborDist(gt_nn, Nneighbors = TRUE)
```

## Comparison

GD returns n-2 values (drops first/last on some chromosomes); gtars returns
one per region.

```{r cmp-nn}
cat("GD:", length(gd_nn), "  gtars:", length(gt_nn), "\n")
plotNeighborDist(
  list("GenomicDistributions" = as.integer(gd_nn), "gtars" = gt_nn),
  Nneighbors = TRUE
)
```


# Feature distance distribution {.tabset}

`calcFeatureDist` computes the signed distance from each region midpoint to
the nearest feature midpoint.

## GenomicDistributions

The tutorial uses `calcFeatureDistRefTSS` to auto-load hg19 TSS annotations:

```{r gd-featdist}
gd_tss <- calcFeatureDistRefTSS(query_gr, "hg19")
plotFeatureDist(gd_tss, featureName = "TSS")
```

## gtars RegionSet

gtars needs explicit TSS GRanges. We build width-1 TSS regions from the
gene starts and convert to RegionSet:

```{r gt-featdist}
tss_gr <- GenomicRanges::promoters(geneModels$genesGR, upstream = 0,
                                    downstream = 1)
tss_rs <- RegionSet(tss_gr)
gt_tss <- gtars::calcFeatureDist(query, tss_rs)

# gtars returns NA for regions on chromosomes without features
gt_tss_valid <- gt_tss[!is.na(gt_tss)]
cat(length(gt_tss), "total,", sum(is.na(gt_tss)), "NAs,",
    length(gt_tss_valid), "with distances\n")
plotFeatureDist(gt_tss_valid, featureName = "TSS")
```

## Comparison

```{r cmp-featdist}
plotFeatureDist(
  list("GenomicDistributions" = gd_tss, "gtars" = gt_tss_valid),
  featureName = "TSS"
)
```

> **Note**: ~2.6% of regions may differ by 1bp due to midpoint rounding
> conventions (see Known Differences below). gtars returns `NA` for regions
> on chromosomes without features; GD computes cross-chromosome distances.

##

Multi-query (GD):

```{r featdist-multi}
gd_tss2 <- calcFeatureDistRefTSS(queryList, "hg19")
plotFeatureDist(gd_tss2, featureName = "TSS")
```

Custom features — randomly shifted copy as features:

```{r featdist-custom}
featureExample <- GenomicRanges::shift(
  query_gr, round(rnorm(length(query_gr), 0, 1000))
)
gd_custom <- GenomicDistributions::calcFeatureDist(query_gr, featureExample)
gt_custom <- gtars::calcFeatureDist(query, RegionSet(featureExample))
gt_custom_valid <- gt_custom[!is.na(gt_custom)]

plotFeatureDist(
  list("GenomicDistributions" = gd_custom, "gtars" = gt_custom_valid)
)
```


# Partition distribution {.tabset}

`calcPartitions` classifies each region into genomic partitions (promoter,
exon, intron, intergenic, etc.).

## GenomicDistributions

```{r gd-partition}
gd_parts <- calcPartitionsRef(query_gr, "hg19")
print(gd_parts)
plotPartitions(gd_parts)
```

## gtars RegionSet

Build the partition list from GD's gene model GRanges. Optionally pass
`chromSizes` to trim promoters at chromosome boundaries:

```{r gt-partition}
chromSizes <- GenomeInfoDb::seqlengths(GenomeInfoDb::Seqinfo(genome = "hg19"))
chromSizes <- chromSizes[grep("^chr[0-9]+$|^chr[XY]$", names(chromSizes))]

gt_pl <- gtars::genomePartitionList(
  geneModels$genesGR, geneModels$exonsGR,
  geneModels$threeUTRGR, geneModels$fiveUTRGR,
  chromSizes = chromSizes
)
gt_parts <- gtars::calcPartitions(query, gt_pl)
print(gt_parts)
plotPartitions(gt_parts)
```

## Comparison

gtars returns a `Freq` column (matching GD), so the outputs merge
directly:

```{r cmp-partition}
merged <- merge(gd_parts, gt_parts, by = "partition",
                suffixes = c("_GD", "_gtars"), all = TRUE)
merged[is.na(merged)] <- 0
print(merged)
```

##

Multi-query with raw numbers (GD):

```{r partition-multi}
gd_parts2 <- calcPartitionsRef(queryList, "hg19")
tryCatch(
  print(plotPartitions(gd_parts2, numbers = TRUE)),
  error = function(e) {
    cat("plotPartitions multi-query failed (known GD/ggplot2 compat issue):\n")
    print(gd_parts2)
  }
)
```


# Expected partition enrichment {.tabset}

`calcExpectedPartitions` compares observed partition overlaps to what would
be expected by chance, using a chi-square test.

## GenomicDistributions

```{r gd-expected}
gd_exp <- calcExpectedPartitionsRef(query_gr, "hg19")
print(gd_exp[, c("partition", "observed", "expected", "log10OE")])
plotExpectedPartitions(gd_exp)
```

## gtars RegionSet

gtars needs explicit chromosome sizes (reusing `chromSizes` from above):

```{r gt-expected}
gt_exp <- gtars::calcExpectedPartitions(query, gt_pl, chromSizes)
print(gt_exp)
```

## Comparison

```{r cmp-expected}
merged_exp <- merge(
  gd_exp[, c("partition", "log10OE")],
  gt_exp[, c("partition", "log10OE")],
  by = "partition", suffixes = c("_GD", "_gtars")
)
print(merged_exp)
```

##

Multi-query with p-values (GD):

```{r expected-multi}
gd_exp2 <- calcExpectedPartitionsRef(queryList, "hg19")
tryCatch(
  print(plotExpectedPartitions(gd_exp2, pval = TRUE)),
  error = function(e) {
    cat("plotExpectedPartitions multi-query failed (known GD/ggplot2 compat issue):\n")
    print(gd_exp2[, c("partition", "observed", "expected", "log10OE")])
  }
)
```


# Cumulative partition distribution

```{r cumulative}
cp <- calcCumulativePartitionsRef(query_gr, "hg19")
plotCumulativePartitions(cp)
```

```{r cumulative-multi}
cp2 <- calcCumulativePartitionsRef(queryList, "hg19")
plotCumulativePartitions(cp2)
```

> **gtars note**: `calcCumulativePartitions` is not yet implemented in gtars.


# Open signal in regions {.tabset}

`calcSummarySignal` overlaps query regions with a signal matrix and computes
per-condition summaries.

```{r signal-setup}
cellMatrix <- data.table::fread(
  system.file("extdata", "example_cell_matrix.txt",
              package = "GenomicDistributions")
)
```

## GenomicDistributions

```{r gd-signal}
gd_sig <- GenomicDistributions::calcSummarySignal(query_gr, cellMatrix)
plotSummarySignal(gd_sig)
```

## gtars RegionSet

```{r gt-signal}
gt_sig <- gtars::calcSummarySignal(query, cellMatrix)
plotSummarySignal(gt_sig)
```

##

Multi-query (GD):

```{r signal-multi}
gd_sig2 <- GenomicDistributions::calcSummarySignal(queryList, cellMatrix)
plotSummarySignal(gd_sig2)
```


# GC content {.tabset}

`calcGCContent` computes the proportion of G/C bases in each region.
GD uses a BSgenome object; gtars uses a FASTA file loaded via
`loadGenomeAssembly`.

```{r gc-setup}
hg19_fasta <- "hg19.fa"
has_fasta <- file.exists(hg19_fasta)
has_bsgenome <- requireNamespace("BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg19", quietly = TRUE)

if (has_fasta) {
  genome_assembly <- gtars::loadGenomeAssembly(hg19_fasta)
  cat("Loaded hg19 FASTA for gtars\n")
} else {
  cat("hg19.fa not found — gtars GC/dinucleotide sections will be skipped\n")
}
```

## GenomicDistributions

```{r gd-gc, eval=has_bsgenome}
gd_gc <- calcGCContentRef(query_gr, "hg19")
plotGCContent(gd_gc)
```

```{r gd-gc-skip, eval=!has_bsgenome, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat("> **Skipped**: `BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg19` is not installed.",
    "Install with `BiocManager::install('BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg19')`.\n")
```

## gtars RegionSet

```{r gt-gc, eval=has_fasta}
gt_gc <- gtars::calcGCContent(query, genome_assembly, ignoreUnkChroms = TRUE)
cat(length(gt_gc), "GC values, range:", round(range(gt_gc), 4), "\n")
plotGCContent(gt_gc)
```

```{r gt-gc-skip, eval=!has_fasta, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat("> **Skipped**: `hg19.fa` not found in test directory.\n")
```

## Comparison

```{r cmp-gc, eval=has_bsgenome && has_fasta}
cat("Max absolute difference:", max(abs(gd_gc - gt_gc)), "\n")
cat("Correlation:", cor(gd_gc, gt_gc), "\n")
plotGCContent(list("GenomicDistributions" = gd_gc, "gtars" = gt_gc))
```

```{r cmp-gc-skip, eval=!(has_bsgenome && has_fasta), echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat("> **Skipped**: Need both BSgenome and hg19.fa for comparison.\n")
```


# Dinucleotide frequency {.tabset}

`calcDinuclFreq` counts all 16 dinucleotide pairs across the query regions.
Like GC content, it requires genome sequence data.

## GenomicDistributions

```{r gd-dinucl, eval=has_bsgenome}
gd_df <- calcDinuclFreqRef(query_gr, "hg19")
plotDinuclFreq(gd_df)
```

```{r gd-dinucl-skip, eval=!has_bsgenome, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat("> **Skipped**: `BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg19` is not installed.\n")
```

## gtars RegionSet

```{r gt-dinucl, eval=has_fasta}
gt_df <- gtars::calcDinuclFreq(query, genome_assembly)
plotDinuclFreq(gt_df)
```

```{r gt-dinucl-skip, eval=!has_fasta, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat("> **Skipped**: `hg19.fa` not found in test directory.\n")
```

## Comparison

```{r cmp-dinucl, eval=has_bsgenome && has_fasta}
plotDinuclFreq(list("GenomicDistributions" = gd_df, "gtars" = gt_df))
```

```{r cmp-dinucl-skip, eval=!(has_bsgenome && has_fasta), echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat("> **Skipped**: Need both BSgenome and hg19.fa for comparison.\n")
```


# Summary

```{r summary-table, echo=FALSE}
coverage <- data.frame(
  `GD function` = c(
    "calcWidth / plotQTHist",
    "calcChromBins / plotChromBins",
    "calcNeighborDist / plotNeighborDist",
    "calcNearestNeighbors",
    "calcFeatureDist / plotFeatureDist",
    "calcPartitions / plotPartitions",
    "calcExpectedPartitions",
    "calcCumulativePartitions",
    "calcSummarySignal / plotSummarySignal (single-query)",
    "calcGCContent / plotGCContent",
    "calcDinuclFreq / plotDinuclFreq"
  ),
  `RegionSet method` = c(
    "widths(rs)",
    "distribution(rs, nBins)",
    "neighborDistances(rs)",
    "nearestNeighbors(rs)",
    "calcFeatureDist(rs, features_rs)",
    "calcPartitions(rs, partitionList)",
    "calcExpectedPartitions(rs, partitionList, chromSizes)",
    "Not yet implemented",
    "calcSummarySignal(rs, signalMatrix)",
    "calcGCContent(rs, genomeAssembly)",
    "calcDinuclFreq(rs, genomeAssembly)"
  ),
  `Status` = c(
    "Exact match",
    "Karyotype-sorted; plotChromBins compatible",
    "Match (also returns signed overlaps)",
    "Match (returns n; GD returns n-2)",
    "Match (see known differences)",
    "Freq column; plotPartitions compatible",
    "plotExpectedPartitions compatible",
    "Not yet implemented",
    "plotSummarySignal compatible",
    "Uses FASTA via loadGenomeAssembly",
    "Uses FASTA via loadGenomeAssembly"
  ),
  check.names = FALSE,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
knitr::kable(coverage, caption = "RegionSet S4 methods vs GenomicDistributions")
```

### Known differences

1. **neighborDistances**: gtars returns signed distances for all consecutive
   pairs; GD filters out negative (overlapping) distances.
2. **nearestNeighbors**: gtars returns one value per region; GD drops
   first/last on some chromosomes.
3. **calcFeatureDist**: ~2.6% of regions may differ by 1bp due to midpoint
   rounding conventions. gtars returns `NA` for regions on chromosomes
   without features; GD computes cross-chromosome distances.
4. **calcExpectedPartitions**: gtars uses a goodness-of-fit (O-E)^2/E
   chi-square formula. R's `chisq.test()` (used by GD) computes a 2x2 test
   of independence and may apply Yates' continuity correction, so p-values
   will differ.
5. **Genome sequence functions**: gtars loads genome from FASTA files via
   `loadGenomeAssembly()` instead of BSgenome objects. Both `calcGCContent`
   and `calcDinuclFreq` accept the resulting pointer. No `*Ref` convenience
   wrappers yet (you must supply the FASTA path yourself).
